acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Upgrade support utilities no longer live in `beads-cli`
- [ ] `beads-cli` cleanly matches the “command language + presentation” boundary
- [ ] `beads-rs` public surface is limited to assembly-level entrypoints/helpers
- [ ] Temporary wrappers introduced during the move are removed before closeout
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-cli -p beads-rs` passes
description:
**Problem**
`beads-rs` is still broader than an assembly crate should be, and `beads-cli` still owns some package/bootstrap policy that is not really part of the `bd` command language. In particular, `crates/beads-cli/src/upgrade/**` currently holds upgrade support utilities that are really about shipping and replacing the installed binary.

**Where**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/upgrade.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/cli/mod.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs`
- `crates/beads-cli/src/upgrade/mod.rs`
- `crates/beads-cli/src/upgrade/release.rs`
- `crates/beads-cli/src/lib.rs`

**Why It Matters**
The end-state boundary for `beads-cli` is command language + presentation. The end-state boundary for `beads-rs` is assembly/package wiring. Until those are separated cleanly, readers still need to reverse-engineer which crate really owns bootstrap/package policy.

**Files to Study**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/upgrade.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs`
- `crates/beads-cli/src/cli.rs`
- `crates/beads-cli/src/backend.rs`
- `crates/beads-cli/src/upgrade/**`
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-final-crate-boundary-cleanup-design.md`
design: Move self-upgrade support out of `beads-cli` into assembly-owned code. Keep `beads-cli` focused on clap grammar, CLI-only validation, argv-to-request lowering, rendering, and host/backend traits. Finish shrinking `beads-rs` so its public API is limited to same-package binary entrypoints and assembly wiring.
title: Move upgrade support out of beads-cli and finish shrinking beads-rs
